title: dipterocarpus retusus var. macrocarpus
intro: Dipterocarpus retusus var. macrocarpus, also known as the giant dipterocarp, is a large tree species native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, broad canopy and long, straight trunk. The tree has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and leaves, which are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of D. retusus var. macrocarpus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve boiling the bark and leaves in water to create a decoction, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as coughs, colds, and skin infections.
